mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the equity discount rate, directly increasing the present value of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which immediately triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which constrains corporate capacity utilization and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4%, or the mechanism shows positive predictive power during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels force central banks to maintain restrictive monetary policy, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205, or the threshold signal loses statistical significance after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings growth, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as macroeconomic expansion sustains equity valuations.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial output alongside stagnant housing construction signals a synchronized slowdown in real economic activity, which triggers immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: The supply-side slowdown signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when recession probability models indicate <10% likelihood, or forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances.
